description: Fully developed physically or mentally; having reached an advanced stage of development. 'Seroli' names the quality of ripeness—fruit ready to eat, person ready to act wisely, process that has reached its fullness.
sound_symbolism: The word settles into fullness. 'Se' begins settled, development advanced. Then 'ro' has grown solid, form achieved. Finally 'li' lives fully, maturity reached.
grammatical_notes: Functions as adjective: 'seroli lurekoi' (mature fruit). As verb: 'seroli' (to mature, to ripen). Between 'limu' (young) and 'serao' (old)—the stage of full development.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Knowing when fruit is mature, when grain is ready—traditional knowledge of ripeness and timing.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Mature practice comes from sustained effort—wisdom and compassion that have ripened through experience.
